summary: I installed the missing gems into a local `vendor/bundle` (matching another worktree's setup), then ran `bin/rubocop` (145 files, no offenses) and the Minitest suite. Running tests with default parallel workers segfaulted in the `pg` gem's native `connect_start` — a fork-safety issue in this Ruby/libpq environment unrelated to the code — so I reran serially (`PARALLEL_WORKERS=0`), which passed cleanly (184 runs, 660 assertions, 0 failures/errors) after one flaky run with transient Postgres "cached plan" errors that didn't reproduce. While verifying the compiled CSS, I found a real, blocking bug the test suite couldn't catch: in `app/assets/tailwind/application.css`, a comment containing "raw gray-*/white" has a stray `*/` that prematurely closes the comment, which swallows the entire light-mode `:root` block defining the default semantic color tokens (`--color-app`, `--color-surface`, `--color-border`, `--color-text`, etc.) — confirmed by checking the compiled `tailwind.css`, where only the `.dark` overrides exist and the light-mode defaults are missing entirely. This breaks styling app-wide for the default (light/system) theme across every view that was swept to use the new semantic tokens. I wrote `verdict.json` with `verdict: needs_work` and one blocker finding describing this.
